LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ARE DENIED. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES WHETHER PLAINTIFF IS DISABLED AND WHETHER DEFENDANT DISCRIMINATED AND/OR RETALIATED AGAINST PLAINTIFF BY PLACING HER ON LEAVE IN JUNE 2009 (I.E. DEFENDANT'S ASSERTED NON-DISCRIMINATORY REASONS WERE PRETEXTUAL). VIEWED FAVORABLY TO PLAINTIFF, THE EVIDENCE SHOWS THAT (1) DR. MANDELL RELEASED PLAINTIFF FOR WORK IN DECEMBER 2008, (2) DEFENDANT ACCEPTED THAT RELEASE AND PLAINTIFF BEGAN WORKING AGAIN, (3) SIX MONTHS LATER WHEN PLAINTIFF RAISED CONCERNS ABOUT THE DANGER OF THE GGGP JOB TRAINING ASSIGNMENT AND HER EMOTIONAL WELL-BEING, DEFENDANT FOUND DR. MANDELL'S DECEMBER 2008 RELEASE UNCERTAIN AND PUT PLAINTIFF ON FORCED LEAVE, AND (4) DEFENDANT DELAYED ALLOWING PLAINTIFF TO RETURN TO WORK. ORDER SIGNED. ORDER RE: PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: NICHOLE RODICH, CSR #11604
JOINT RESPONSE TO OSC RE: MEDAIRATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF MATTSON, CANDIE

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-01-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES.
Appearances: Karine Bohbot of Bohbot & Riles (510.273.3111) for plaintiff and responding party; Larry Hecimovich of the Office of the City Attorney (554.3933) for defendant and moving party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: the motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. There are triable issues whether Plaintiff is disabled and whether Defendant discriminated and/or retaliated against Plaintiff by placing her on leave in June 2009 (i.e. Defendant's asserted non-discriminatory reasons were pretextual). Viewed favorably to Plaintiff, the evidence shows that (1) Dr. Mandell released Plaintiff for work in December 2008, (2) Defendant accepted that release and Plaintiff began working again, (3) six months later when Plaintiff raised concerns about the danger of the GGGP Job Training Assignment and her emotional well-being, Defendant found Dr. Mandell's December 2008 release uncertain and put Plaintiff on forced leave, and (4) Defendant delayed allowing Plaintiff to return to work. Order signed. Order re: plaintiff's objections signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Nichole Rodich, CSR #11604
